title: Trump Administration to Impose New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
summary: The administration announced new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a global 10 percent tariff set to expire at midnight.

title: Stocks Tumble as A.I. Spending and Oil Prices Weigh on Market
summary: The S&P 500 tumbled more than 1 percent, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite sank more than 2 percent.

title: U.S. Withdraws Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
summary: A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times called an attempt to intimidate the free press.

title: Kagan Says Supreme Court Has Stood Up to Trump in Key Cases
summary: At an annual meeting of judges and lawyers, the liberal justice defended her conservative colleagues from criticism that they have rubber-stamped President Trump’s agenda.

title: Trump to Impose Tariffs on More Than 80 Countries
summary: Also, oil hits $100 a barrel. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.

title: A Fallen Soldier’s Angst, Love and Longing to Return Home From the Iran War
summary: Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton was killed in recent days, one of 18 U.S. soldiers who have lost their lives since the beginning of the war in Iran.

title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

title: House Rebukes Trump on Iran in War Powers Vote as Senate Blocks Debate
summary: For the second time, a few House Republicans joined Democrats to back a measure calling on the president to seek authorization for the war.

title: For South Korea, U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Exposes a Double Standard
summary: President Trump has put Saudi Arabia on the path to enriching uranium on its own soil, a demand South Korea has been making for years.

title: Did the World Cup Live Up to Tourism Expectations? Here Are the Winners and Losers.
summary: Host cities, hotels, tourism boards and more had lofty expectations for the World Cup, but some struck gold, while some fizzled. Here’s the breakdown.

title: Trump and Thune Clash Over SAVE America Act
summary: A White House spokeswoman said President Trump’s patience was running thin. The top Senate Republican said she should try finding the votes.

title: Democratic Voters Keep Defying the Party Establishment
summary: The House Democratic campaign arm has stepped up its interventions in high-profile contested primaries. But this week, it suffered its third primary defeat in two months.

title: Why I Sought Out a Taliban Commander I Had Fought
summary: First as a Marine and then as a journalist, I spent years tied to Afghanistan. I returned one last time to speak to a former enemy in search of answers, and perhaps closure.

title: 2 Men Stabbed on Upper West Side in Possible Hate Crimes, Police Say
summary: A man was arrested after the daylight attacks in a Manhattan neighborhood bordering Central Park. The police were investigating whether the stabbings were hate crimes.

title: The Defining Image of India’s Gen Z Protests
summary: A new protest movement is spreading across India, and the video of a woman blocking a police van has captured the resolve of the young people leading it.

title: Prominent Indian Activist Breaks Hunger Strike After 26 Days
summary: The activist, Sonam Wangchuk, said that he had broken his fast “after long negotiations” with officials and “in view of possible violence in the country.”

title: Modi Caught Off Guard as India’s Economic Failings Inflame Young People
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was busy trying to put out other fires when a student movement suddenly exploded. There are no easy solutions to the problems behind it.

title: The Tate Brothers Are Losing Right-Wing Allies After Their Arrest
summary: After Andrew and Tristan Tate, prominent leaders of the so-called manosphere, were arrested in Miami, many of their onetime allies on the American right pulled back support.

title: Los Angeles Homeless Population Grows Again, After 2 Years of Decline
summary: Los Angeles had an increase in homeless residents between early 2025 and early 2026. The rise could hurt Mayor Karen Bass as she runs for re-election.

title: A Lakers Jacket, Bought for $3 at Goodwill, Fetches Nearly $90,000 at Auction
summary: Quinn Brown, a 19-year-old thrifter from Portland, Ore., bought a warm-up jacket worn by Wilt Chamberlain for $3.07. Six months later, Sotheby’s auctioned it off for $89,600.

title: Gen Z Fell Out of Love With Work. I Don’t Blame Them.
summary: For Gen Z college graduates, the way millennials lived out their ambitions no longer makes sense.

title: How Trump and His Allies Can Corrupt the Midterms
summary: Stop the steal before it starts.

title: Bear Dies After Getting Stranded Atop Electric Pole in New Mexico
summary: The animal’s plight alarmed onlookers and highlighted the drought conditions that are forcing wild bears closer to where people live in search of food.
